content: The SOC received an alert about a spike in DNS queries to random domains from workstation FIN-WS22. Help with the initial investigation.
Host FIN-WS22 started becoming active around 02:13 UTC. Queries appeared every 45 seconds.
There is a powershell.exe spawned by winword.exe exactly 2 minutes before the beaconing started.
I am conducting an internal pentest and found a login endpoint that responds differently for valid and invalid usernames.
The valid username gives a 200 response with the message "password incorrect". The invalid username gives a 404.
There is no lockout. All requests are accepted.
There is a SIEM alert for possible lateral movement using PsExec on the server subnet.
Account svc-backup from APP-12 to DB-03 around 03:41 AM.
There is no ticket. Usually that account only accesses backup file shares.
There is a new service named PSEXESVC and binary random.exe in ADMIN$.
The internal web server experienced a sudden CPU spike after deploying a new application.
There is a process named xmrig using almost 90% of the CPU.
The binary was executed by www-data from a child process of the upload application.
